last_concepts: market_reaction: Alphabet's $80B equity raise (upsized to $84.75B) to fund AI compute, GOOGL ~-4%, Berkshire anchoring $10B, ~1.8% dilution on a ~$4.5T cap; the richest company on earth issued fresh stock to chase AI and diluted every holder while the market dropped it the same day

educational: A company and its stock are two different things — you can buy the best business (record earnings, real product) and still lose, because you bought the price and the price already knew; you're buying the crowd's opinion, not the business

hot_take: The trader, not the strategy, is the broken variable — the edge isn't a better setup, it's removing the one variable that hopes, panics, and moves the stop; a system runs the same plan a thousand times without a bad morning, and the hardest trade is the one without you in it
